The official trail map warns that some routes cross private land and users must stay on marked trails.

known_limits: No live muddy-trail, gate, or temporary-closure feed is connected.

destinations.name: Scout Mountain Campground
destinations.description: A forest campground near Pocatello that can work well for a simple mountain overnight when seasonal access is open.

coordinates.precision: official-source coordinates reviewed

facilities.water: drinking water listed; verify seasonal service

current.status: Official static facts reviewed where possible; live trip-day checks still required

verified_facts: Scout Mountain Campground is about 13 miles south of Pocatello at roughly 6,900 feet.
Official listings describe paved access with switchbacks and RV access.
First-come and reservable sites, vault toilets, and drinking water are listed.
There are no utility hookups, and visitors must pack out garbage.
known_limits: Dogs may be compatible with campsites, but the adjacent Scout Lake Day Use Area has a separate no-dog rule.
Opening dates, water service, fire restrictions, and availability require a live check.

verified_facts: Palisades Reservoir is managed by the Bureau of Reclamation with recreation administered by the Caribou-Targhee National Forest.
Official information describes roughly 25 square miles of water and about 70 miles of shoreline.
Public recreation includes camping, picnicking, boat ramps, boating, and fishing.
known_limits: Fluctuating water levels can affect ramps, shoreline access, boating, and fishing.
Facilities and rules vary by access point.

destinations.name: Bear Lake State Park

verified_facts: Bear Lake State Park has multiple units with different camping, parking, beach, reservation, and dog rules.
North Beach does not allow dogs.
Mountain Fawn and Sage Springs allow dogs when confined or on a leash no longer than six feet.
Mountain Fawn campsites are reservation-only.
known_limits: Recommendations must name the specific park unit; the whole park cannot safely be labeled simply dog-friendly.
Beach capacity, parking, water, fire rules, and campsite availability change.

destinations.name: Minnetonka Cave

verified_facts: Minnetonka Cave is a guided-tour cave with 888 stairs.
The cave stays near 40°F year-round.
Potable water is not available.
White-nose syndrome safeguards prohibit items previously used in another cave or mine.
known_limits: Tours, tickets, opening, and hours require a current official check.
The official page contains older seasonal-hour text, so hours are intentionally not embedded.

verified_facts: Henrys Lake State Park is a high-elevation fishing and camping park about 15 miles west of Yellowstone National Park.
The official page lists 83 serviced sites and three cabins.
Verified ADA features include a boat ramp, cabin, campsite, restroom, and trail.
The park lists cutthroat, brook, and cut-bow hybrid trout.
known_limits: High-elevation weather changes quickly.
Water, dump station, winter services, boating, and availability require current checks.
